summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

A parking garage charges $5 for the first hour and $3 for each additional hour. What is the cost for 4 hours?

First hour: $5. Additional 3 hours: 3($3) = $9. Total: $14.

A tax system charges 10% on income up to $10,000 and 20% above that. What is the tax on $25,000?

First $10,000 at 10%: $1,000. Next $15,000 at 20%: $3,000. Total: $4,000.

A phone plan: $30/month for up to 5 GB, then $10 per additional GB. What is the cost for 8 GB?

Base $30 for 5 GB + 3 extra GB at $10 each = $30. Total: $60.

A rideshare app charges $2/mile normally and $3/mile when demand ≥ 80%. During 90% demand, what is a 10-mile cost?

At 90% (≥ 80%), rate is $3/mile. 10 miles: $30.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: A parking garage charges $5 for the first hour and $3 for each additional hour. What is the cost for 4 hours?

First hour: $5. Additional 3 hours: 3($3) = $9. Total: $14.

Example: Water utility: first 100 gal free, $0.005/gal for 101–1000, $0.01/gal above 1000. Usage charge for 1,500 gallons?

First 100: $0. Next 900: 900(0.005) = $4.50. Next 500: 500(0.01) = $5. Total: $9.50.
